title: notholaena hirsuta var. glabra
intro: Notholaena hirsuta var. glabra, also known as the hairless woolly fern, is a type of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Notholaena hirsuta var. glabra include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as acting as a natural antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a salve or ointment.
